summary: Large mnitemsw decomp/matching PR adding substantial C implementation and header updates for the menu item switch unit. Bot report credits three new 100% matches and several near-matches/improvements in main/melee/mn/mnitemsw, including code and data section progress.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source change; adds includes, externs, static table data, helper functions, menu input handling, cursor/item animation updates, and matching tactics.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/mn/mnitemsw.c added 812 deleted 0 hunks 2
raw_diff: mnitemsw.c adds MnItemSwTable, mnItemSw_803ED340, mnItemSw_804D4BA0, mnItemSw_80233A98, mnItemSw_80233B68, fn_80233E10, mnItemSw_80234104, mnItemSw_8023453C, fn_80234C24 excerpt
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: main/melee/mn/mnitemsw received 3 new matches and 8 improvements

key_files.role: Header updated alongside source work; exact declarations not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/melee/mn/mnitemsw.h added 7 deleted 6 hunks 1

what_changed: Added many dependencies to mnitemsw.c, including gobj/gobjplink/sislib/debug/memory, gm, lb, language, mnmainrule, and sc type headers.
Introduced extern declarations for MenMainCursorIs_Top, MenMainConIs_Top, mnItemSw_804D6BE8, and mn_804D6BB5 in the source excerpt.
Added struct MnItemSwTable with x00, x30, items, and item_order arrays, plus static table mnItemSw_803ED340 and small f32 table mnItemSw_804D4BA0.
Implemented mnItemSw_80233A98 with JP/non-JP language-dependent item animation/id remapping for cases 17 and 19.
Implemented mnItemSw_80233B68 to update hovered_selection and confirmed_selection for directional inputs, including special entries 0x1F and 0x20.
Implemented fn_80233E10 to process back/A/start/buttons, persist item settings through mn_8022E978 and gmMainLib_8015CC58()->item_freq, play menu SFX, and transition/close menu objects.
Added animation/cursor update routines including mnItemSw_80234104 and mnItemSw_8023453C using JObj flag, animation, translation, and TOBJ loop helpers.
Bot report says new 100% matches: mnItemSw_80233B68 (+680 bytes), mnItemSw_80235020 (+384 bytes), and mnItemSw_80233A98 (+208 bytes).
Bot report says near-match/improvement progress for mnItemSw_802351A0, mnItemSw_8023453C, mnItemSw_80234104, fn_80234C24, fn_80233E10, .data, .sdata2, and .sdata.
smart_moves: Grouped related animation/item order constants into a local MnItemSwTable instead of scattering raw arrays through the functions.
Used natural loops for repeated item application and JObj updates, especially 0x1F item loops over item_order and data->items.
Preserved special cursor states 0x1F and 0x20 explicitly, making the all-items/frequency-style selection paths searchable and reviewable.
Kept still-unknown functions with address-style names such as fn_80233E10 and fn_80234C24 rather than inventing semantics unsupported by the excerpt.

Used existing JObj and menu helpers such as HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, lb_80011E24, mn_8022F3D8, and mn_8022ED6C instead of raw pointer operations in the shown code.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For menu tables, a temporary local struct can make clustered animation constants, item ids, and item ordering easier to use consistently across multiple recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct MnItemSwTable with x00, x30, items, item_order
raw_diff: mnItemSw_80234104 and mnItemSw_8023453C access tbl->x30, tbl->item_order, and tbl->items

decomp_lessons.lesson: Directional menu navigation often needs explicit edge/sentinel cases before the default increment/decrement path; here 0, 15, 16, 30, 31, and 32 receive special handling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnItemSw_80233B68 switch cases for left/right and special handling for 0x1F/0x20

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated menu setting persistence is naturally expressed as a for loop over the item_order table and data->items, matching the loaded natural-loops standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80233E10 repeats for (i = 0; i < 0x1F; i++) mn_8022E978(mnItemSw_803ED340.item_order[i], data->items[i])
decomp_standards:natural-loops

decomp_lessons.lesson: Keep language-region behavior local and explicit when it changes item animation/id mapping; mnItemSw_80233A98 branches on lbLang_IsSettingJP for selected cases.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mnItemSw_80233A98 cases 17 and 19 use lbLang_IsSettingJP and return non-JP constants 0x28/0x27

naming_conventions.convention: Observed use of address-style global/function names for unresolved symbols: mnItemSw_803ED340, mnItemSw_804D4BA0, mnItemSw_804D6BE8, fn_80233E10, fn_80234C24.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added extern/static/function declarations with address-style names

naming_conventions.convention: Observed short role locals where roles are clear, such as data, buttons, hovered, sel, cursor, old_cursor, new_cursor, cjobj, jobj, text, menu_changed, hovered_changed, confirmed_changed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local variable declarations in mnItemSw_80233B68, fn_80233E10, mnItemSw_80234104, mnItemSw_8023453C, fn_80234C24

naming_conventions.convention: Unclear struct fields remain address/offset-style, such as x00, x30, x21, x23, instead of guessed semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct MnItemSwTable fields x00/x30 and MnItemSwData field accesses x21/x23
raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline on before mnItemSw_80233A98 and #pragma dont_inline reset after it
text_corpus.jsonl bot report: mnItemSw_80233A98 is a new 100% match

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: May reflect MWCC codegen sensitivity for table iteration; no reviewer explanation loaded.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence supports the code shape only; no type-ownership conclusion should be promoted from this PR alone.

accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR groups mnitemsw animation/item constants into MnItemSwTable and uses loops over item_order/data->items for repeated menu persistence and animation setup, aligning with the natural-loops standard.

raw_diff: for loops over 0x1F item_order entries

source_update_candidates.title: Cautionary example: pragma/PAD_STACK tactics need narrow evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2389 contains scoped dont_inline around a newly matched function and PAD_STACK in improved-but-not-fully-matched functions. It can serve as an example that such tactics should stay narrow and be tied to bot/objdiff evidence rather than treated as ordinary style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma dont_inline around mnItemSw_80233A98
raw_diff: PAD_STACK in fn_80233E10 and fn_80234C24
text_corpus.jsonl bot report
source_update_candidates.reason: This reinforces existing matching-tactics/pragma standards, but loaded evidence is insufficient for a new global rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that #pragma dont_inline or PAD_STACK are generally acceptable style from this PR; only narrow PR-local match/improvement evidence is loaded.
Do not claim mnItemSw_8023453C, mnItemSw_80234104, fn_80234C24, fn_80233E10, or mnItemSw_802351A0 fully matched; bot report lists them as improvements below 100%.
Do not promote exact mnitemsw.h declaration facts from this slice; the header hunk is not visible in loaded excerpts.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale; no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
